---
title: ggplot2
subtitle: Day 03
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: ../img/02-ggplot-layers.png
  data-background-size: 60%
  data-background-position: right
  data-background-opacity: "0.8"
  data-slide-number: none
format: 
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
auto-stretch: false
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

library(countdown)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(ymlthis)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(patchwork)

slides_theme = theme_minimal(
  base_family = "Atkinson Hyperlegible",
  base_size = 20)
theme_set(slides_theme)
```

## A Note on Assignments

Most things listed under the Due column on the schedule (HWs, portfolios, forms, project check-ins) are due at 11:59pm

Final project due at noon

You will submit HWs on BOTH GitHub (commit and push) AND Gradescope (upload pdf)

- You can also see the README file (which provides the "rubric" for HWs) on both GitHub and Gradescope

## Plan for today:

1.  Quick grammar of graphics recap
2.  Speed Groupwork

## What are the *essential* components of this plot?

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false


ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) + 
  geom_point()
```

# Data {background-image="../img/02-ggplot-layers.png" background-size="60%" background-position="right" background-opacity="0.8"}

## Data 

```{r}
#| echo: false
penguins %>%
  knitr::kable() %>%
  kableExtra::kable_paper("hover", full_width = F, position = "float_left") %>%
  kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "70%")
```

## Aesthetic Mapping {background-image="../img/02-ggplot-layers.png" background-size="60%" background-position="right" background-opacity="0.5"}

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
Visual properties that the data is mapped to

-   x → body mass

-   y → flipper length

-   color → species
:::
::::

## Geometric Object {background-image="../img/02-ggplot-layers.png" background-size="60%" background-position="right" background-opacity="0.5"}

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
How the data are represented

-   individual penguins → points
:::
::::

## The basic ggplot template:

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
ggplot(data = <dataset_name>) + 
  <geom_function>(mapping = aes(<mappings>))
```

- data: `data = `
- aesthetic: `mapping = aes(...)`
- geometry: `geom_function`
- Note: aesthetic mappings can also appear in the ggplot function - "global" - passed down to all subsequent geom layers of the plot

## Examples

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| layout-ncol: 2

ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) + 
  geom_point()

# Compare above plot to plot that would result from code below:
#ggplot(penguins) + 
#  geom_point(aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species))

ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g)) + 
  geom_histogram()
```

# Other layers next week!

<!-- ## Facets -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- #| echo: true -->
<!-- ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() +  -->
<!--   facet_wrap(vars(species)) + -->
<!--   theme(legend.position = "none") -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ## Statistical Transformations -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- #| echo: false -->
<!-- #| warning: false -->
<!-- #| message: false -->

<!-- p1 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() + -->
<!--   labs( -->
<!--     title = "Raw Data" -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- p2 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + -->
<!--   labs( -->
<!--     title = "Transformation" -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- p3 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() +  -->
<!--   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + -->
<!--   labs( -->
<!--     title = "Data + Fit" -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- p1 + p2 + p3 & theme(legend.position = "none") -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ## Coordinates {background-image="../img/02-coordinates.png" background-position="right" background-size="50%"} -->

<!-- ::: aside -->
<!-- Source: Nathan Yau, Data Points -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- ## Scales/guides {background-image="../img/02-scales.png" background-position="right" background-size="60%"} -->

<!-- ::: aside -->
<!-- Source: Nathan Yau, Data Points -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- ## Themes -->

<!-- All non-data ink (e.g. background color, appearance of grid lines) -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- #| echo: false -->
<!-- #| warning: false -->
<!-- #| message: false -->


<!-- p1 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() + -->
<!--   theme_gray() -->

<!-- p2 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() + -->
<!--   theme_minimal() -->

<!-- p3 = ggplot(penguins, aes(x = body_mass_g, y = flipper_length_mm, col = species)) +  -->
<!--   geom_point() + -->
<!--   hrbrthemes::theme_ft_rc() -->

<!-- p1 + p2 + p3 & theme(legend.position = "none") -->
<!-- ``` -->

# Speed Group Work {.maize}

## How it works

::: nonincremental

1.  Draw a card
2.  Find your number's location
3.  Introduce yourselves
4.  Work on problems in 03-ggplot-intro.html!
:::

::: callout-important
## Wait!

If you finish the problems in one section, please wait to move on until the next round of groups
:::

## Round 1: View the data + pipes

```{r echo=FALSE}
countdown(8)
```

## Round 2: Scatterplots

```{r echo=FALSE}
countdown(8)
```

## Round 3: Additional Aesthetics

```{r echo=FALSE}
countdown(8)
```

## Round 4: Visualizing Distributions

```{r echo=FALSE}
countdown(8)
```

## Round 5: Bar and column charts + Labeling

```{r echo=FALSE}
countdown(8)
```
